reason: While the text does describe supernatural destructions by Yahweh (fire from heaven consuming military units sent by the king, and bears attacking young men), these are not disasters affecting the general public. The destructions are targeted and specific: (1) individual judgment against King Ahaziah for consulting Baal-Zebub; (2) destruction of two separate military contingents (captains of fifty and their troops) sent specifically to arrest the prophet Elijah—these are royal/military personnel, not the general population; (3) bears attacking specific young men who mocked Elisha. Additionally, the text includes the healing of the waters of Jericho, which is remedial (removing death and barrenness) rather than destructive to the public. The narrative does not depict or prophesy a widespread public disaster, only targeted divine judgments against individuals and specific groups in opposition to God's prophet.
